name: nWave
description: Agentic AI framework that guides developers from idea to working code through seven sequential waves with human gates and strict TDD enforcement inside Claude Code

description.value: Deterministic seven-wave agentic framework running inside Claude Code (or OpenCode), orchestrated by a 40-agent team with a Distributed Enforcement System (DES) that monitors every tool invocation and enforces TDD discipline.

creator.comment: nWave-ai is the GitHub organization; the PyPI package is maintained by Michele Brissoni.

first-release.comment: Earliest PyPI release visible is v1.1.0 on 2026-02-13; no earlier public release found.

pricing-model.comment: MIT-licensed OSS; no paid tier advertised on nwave.ai or the repo. The underlying Claude Code runtime is separate and paid.

covered-phases.comment: Seven waves map as: DISCOVER+DIVERGE=discovery, DISCUSS=spec, DESIGN=architecture (+planning via wave-decisions/recommendation/roadmap artifacts), DEVOPS=deployment, DISTILL=testing (BDD acceptance tests), DELIVER=implementation. Review is explicit via 14 peer-reviewer agents and mandatory reviewer approval before commit. No retrospective phase.

methodology-style.comment: SSOT docs are the central artifact (spec-centric); the seven-wave ordering is core (process-centric); DES enforces Outside-In TDD and refuses commits with failing tests (strict-tdd); DISTILL wave produces Given-When-Then acceptance tests (bdd); 40 distinct agents with specialized roles (multi-agent); organized around agile feature delivery (agent-agile).

opinionated.comment: DES enforces phase ordering, TDD red/green/refactor, and eleven quality gates before any commit. Rigor profiles adjust depth but not the wave sequence.

ceremony-level.comment: Seven waves, 40 agents, mandatory reviewer approvals, eleven quality gates, multiple artifact types (vision, JTBD, journeys, ADRs, user stories, acceptance tests, roadmap). Rigor profiles can lower but not remove this.

mechanization-level.comment: Slash commands (/nw-discover, /nw-deliver, etc.) drive each wave end-to-end with DES hooks mechanically enforcing rules; not a fully managed platform, so not very-high.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Explicit human review between every wave; "no code ships without reviewer approval."

approval-gates.comment: Human approval between DISCUSS (spec), DESIGN (plan/architecture), DISTILL (tasks/acceptance tests), and DELIVER (implementation); no gate at deploy.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Framework is explicitly positioned as human-in-the-loop; DES refuses to advance without approvals.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration requires explicit human approval between each of seven waves; DES enforces TDD red/green mechanically. Closer to phase-level-plus approvals than fully autonomous.

rollback-support.comment: No dedicated rollback command found. Artifacts live in git and can be reverted via normal git operations; DES logs commits but does not provide a first-class undo.

supported-agents.comment: Claude Code is primary; OpenCode IDE is documented alternative at ~67% feature parity. No other agent runtimes officially supported.

editor-integrations.comment: Runs inside Claude Code (terminal) or OpenCode (terminal IDE). No dedicated VS Code/JetBrains/Neovim extensions.

model-agnostic.comment: Primarily targets Claude models (haiku/sonnet/opus) via Claude Code; OpenCode path uses OpenAI models but is explicitly a degraded preview.

mcp-support.comment: No mention of Model Context Protocol integration in README, install guide, or reference docs as of 2026-04-21.

artifact-formats.comment: Vision/briefs/ADRs in Markdown; jobs/journeys/kpi-contracts in YAML; roadmap.json and DES audit logs in JSON/JSONL. Acceptance tests use .feature (Gherkin) which isn't in the schema tag set.

artifacts-covered.comment: docs/product/ includes vision (user-docs), architecture/brief+ADRs (architecture, decisions), jobs+journeys (specs). docs/features/{id}/ adds feature-brief+user-stories (specs), wave-decisions+recommendation (plans), roadmap.json (roadmap), acceptance-tests.feature (test-plans).

project-layout.value: docs/product/ (SSOT: vision, jobs, journeys, architecture, ADRs, KPIs) + docs/features/{id}/ (per-feature deltas) + tests/acceptance/{feature-id}/ (BDD .feature files); DES config in .nwave/des-config.json and logs in .nwave/des/logs/

artifacts-in-git.comment: All artifacts live under docs/ and tests/ in the project repo.

language-agnostic.comment: Framework itself is language-neutral. Example fixtures show Python (conftest.py) but BDD .feature files and the wave ordering apply to any stack.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Agent prompts (instructional); DES match/case hooks via OpenCode's tool.execute.before (hooks); TDD red/green refuses commits with failing tests (test-gates); 14 peer-reviewer agents (agent-review); mandatory human sign-off between waves (manual-approval).

strict-phase-ordering.comment: DES blocks out-of-order progression; Vera orchestrator refuses implementation without approved acceptance criteria.

artifact-validation.comment: Eleven quality gates include architectural boundary enforcement, test budgets, business language verification, and static analysis before any commit; DES also validates file-scope and TDD phase markers.

memory-implementation.comment: SSOT under docs/product/ (markdown+YAML) plus Claude Code's CLAUDE.md convention. No vector DB or knowledge graph; treating the markdown+YAML combination under the markdown-files tag since the schema lacks a combined markdown+yaml option.

persistent-memory.comment: Artifacts are git-tracked, so context survives across sessions without manual handoff.

project-context-files.comment: Uses docs/product/vision.md and related SSOT files, plus Claude Code's CLAUDE.md through the host runtime.

cost-tracking.comment: DES audit trail logs tool invocations (basic-logs). /nw-rigor profiles (lean/standard/thorough/exhaustive/custom) bound the depth of work per wave, functioning as soft budget limits; not per-run token counters.

run-history.comment: DES writes JSONL audit logs (.nwave/des/logs/audit-YYYY-MM-DD.log) of task invocations, TDD phase lifecycle, file-scope violations, and git commits, with daily rotation.

telemetry-opt.comment: Version-check frequency is configurable in des-config.json (daily/weekly/every_session/never). No external analytics telemetry documented.

custom-templates.comment: Reference lists 9 templates; /nw-forge workflow lets users define new agent templates with persona/principles/skills.

hooks.comment: DES hooks fire on tool invocations via OpenCode's tool.execute.before; configurable in .nwave/des-config.json. Primarily for enforcement rather than arbitrary user scripts.

sub-agents.comment: /nw-forge builds specialized agents from scratch; reference documents 42 agents and 195 skills that users can extend.

marketplace.comment: A Claude Code plugin marketplace distribution exists, though it is described as a degraded preview because it lacks DES enforcement hooks.

install-mechanism.comment: Distributed on PyPI; installed via pipx or uv tool (both pip-based). Also available via Claude Code plugin marketplace as a secondary path.

setup-time.comment: pipx/uv install + nwave-ai install + Claude Code restart; no explicit duration quoted but installation guide covers multiple steps, putting it in the 5-30 min band.

self-hosted.comment: Installed locally to ~/.claude/ with per-project .nwave/des-config.json; no nWave-operated cloud service.

requires-account.comment: nWave itself has no account system. The host runtime (Claude Code / OpenCode) requires its own vendor account, but that is separate from the framework.

git-worktree-support.comment: No documented integration with git worktrees found in README, install guide, or reference material as of 2026-04-21.

documentation-quality.comment: 30+ guides split into tutorials/how-to/reference; auto-generated reference for 42 agents, 27 commands, 195 skills, 9 templates. Comprehensive and current (v3.11.0), docked slightly for opacity around MCP/worktree/token-cost topics.

github-stars.comment: Star count captured on 2026-04-21; stales quickly.

active-maintenance.comment: v3.11.0 released 2026-04-16, with 70+ releases since February 2026 — well within the 90-day window.

example-projects.comment: Tutorial series includes first-feature/first-delivery/delivery-pipeline walkthroughs; conference talk 'From Spaghetti to Ravioli' (March 2026) documents real refactoring use. No standalone sample repo linked from README.

community-channel.comment: README links a Discord server as the primary community channel.
